messages.content: Please look into this document and create a summary of 1 paragraph and total costs.

The document outlines Crannull’s phased AI implementation plan prepared by DBWD, aimed at improving efficiency through automation, data handling, and content generation tools [1]. Stage 1, already completed, established a secure AI portal (ai.crannull.co.uk) for consistent, brand‑aligned content production and includes refinements to improve reliability and unify multiple assistants into one. Stage 2 focuses on integrating tools for data manipulation, exporting structured outputs, and syncing live documents from SharePoint/OneDrive for real‑time access and processing, with an estimated development time of 3 weeks plus 1 week of testing. Future stages propose advanced integrations such as CRM linking, AI‑based data cleansing, call transcription summaries, and performance analysis to further enhance business intelligence and automation capabilities.  

**Total Costs:**  
- Stage 1: £1,600 (one‑off)  
- Stage 2: £2,000 (one‑off)  
- Future Stages: To Be Confirmed (TBC)  
**Total (confirmed): £3,600** [1]

document:
AI Implementation & Consultancy 
 
October 2025 
Created for James and Sam 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Prepared by 
Ethan Copping & Danny Barker 
www.dbwd.co.uk 2 
 
Background 
 
Following discussions Crannull would like to implement some AI tools to assist the 
internal team with data cleansing, data sourcing, content creation, reduce 
administration tasks and general time saving. It was highlighted that this could be 
approached in stages, allowing the Crannull team time to use and understand the 
tools, before they become part of everyday usage. 
 
Overall, the AI tools will save staff time, make information easier to find and scale 
up as the business develops. Our initial discussion focused on identifying and 
prioritising AI application usage and use cases that will enhance Crannull's 
efficiency. This phased approach focuses on delivering immediate value whilst 
establishing a competitive advantage and laying the groundwork for more 
advanced AI integrations later down the road. 
  
Current Systems 
 
CRM System: FiveCRM 
Storage System: SharePoint: AU Only 
Storage System: UK Only 
 
Project Title 
 
Crannull AI 3 
 
DBWD Scope of Work 
 
Stage 1 - Custom AI Portal - Completed 
 
Overview: 
We have setup a secure AI portal at ai.crannull.co.uk that lets staff generate 
consistent, brand‑aligned content such as emails, summaries and internal 
knowledge checks via pre‑configured. The plan now is to complete further stages 
and full details about this stage can be found in the previous Crannull – AI 
Portals 2025 June document. 
 
Stage 1 - Refinements: 
 
Usage: 
For improving reliability and consistency in day-to-day use. This refinement 
ensures the assistant clearly states what it can and cannot do, reduces "false 
promises," and streamlines the experience toward a single, capable assistant. It 
also lays lightweight building blocks (basic tools) that Stage 2 will extend. 
 
Approach: 
Users continue to log into ai.crannull.co.uk. We will strengthen the system prompt, 
explicitly list available tools, and consolidate separate assistants (Email / Grammar 
/ General) into one primary assistant that dynamically uses tools. We'll also add 
basic assistive tools such as calculators, time/date and user memories over the 
next two portal updates to support future capabilities. 
 
Costs: 
Included in the current monthly subscription, and no action or payment is required 
by Crannull. This is already in development as part of our testing for the proposal 
and will be rolled out in due course. 4 
 
Stage 2 – Tools & Data Imports 
 
Usage: 
Enable reliable, repeatable "data work" inside the portal: reading and transforming 
spreadsheets, producing ranked tables, and generating formatted outputs 
(PDF/XLSX/DOCX) for example. Also to bring live document sync 
(SharePoint/OneDrive and/or file server) so the assistant can fetch the right 
documents via tools rather than ad‑hoc retrieval augmented generation. 
 
Approach: 
Implement an array of MCP tools that the assistant can call for file/search/export 
actions. Add a live syncing pipeline (SharePoint/OneDrive and/or file server) into a 
server-side index so we can pass relevant documents to the assistant via tools. 
Re‑enable the Internal Assistant as a tool-driven capability (fetching/summarising 
synced docs) rather than a separate model. We will start by pulling from 
cloud.dbwd.co.uk and integrate directly with SharePoint/File Server where access 
is available (via Crannull's IT provider). 
 
Features: 
MCP tools (tool‑calling inside the assistant) to: 
 Read/search structured data from spreadsheets reliably. 
 Perform operations: deduplicate numbers, filter by criteria, build ranked 
tables, etc using bespoke python tools. 
 Generate formatted outputs (PDF, Excel, Word) and provide 
downloadable files. 
 Improve and speed up web searching with MCP-backed retrieval. 
 
SharePoint/OneDrive and/or File Server Sync: 
 Selective, live sync of key folders. e.g. Current Clients & Old Clients. 
 Server-side indexing and metadata to ensure accurate retrieval. 
 Clean separation of folder scopes and automatic updates on 
add/change/remove. 5 
 
Benefits: 
 Reliable, auditable results for database‑style tasks. 
 Faster, clearer workflows for everyday data operations. 
 One assistant that can fetch, transform, and export with sources. 
 Up-to-date knowledge via live sync, without manual uploads. 
 
Costs: 
A one‑off cost of £2,000, taking 3 weeks of development and 1 week of testing. 
During this stage these features will be accessible through a beta model on the 
portal which will sit alongside the main model and can be restricted to admin users 
on request. This is subject to implementation with current Crannull AI systems 
and how the data is pulled. No additional ongoing costs will be added to the 
monthly service. 6 
 
Future Stages – Integrations 
 
Call Transcripts with Templates: 
Meeting notes or call summaries from transcribed calls formatted into structured 
Word/PDF documents using predefined templates and field mapping, with 
validation and human finalisation. Calls will be listened to with AI, with points 
summarised at the end of the conversation and provided to Crannull for review. 
 
CRM Integration: 
API calls into FiveCRM via to enrich answers with live data using RAG and 
database lookups, with guardrails and read-only access. 
 
Data Cleansing: 
AI solution that looks for outdated, duplicated and erroneous entries and 
attempts to populate old or missing data with new data or remove the data is no 
longer viable. Database refinement of 110,000 unique records through removal 
of dormant/obsolete leads, verification of company data accuracy, LinkedIn 
integration for data validation 
 
Sales Performance Analysis: 
AI-driven assessment of call quality and sales methodology for staff 
Development as well as tone and notes of client interactions. 
 
Data Intelligence: 
Automated pre-call research and data gap identification. 
 
Costs:  
We have not included a cost for these stages and serve as ideas of what to next 
progress as the specifics will need to be defined based on each task. 7 
 
 
Timescales 
The timeframes below are to gain an estimate of timescales before beginning 
development. Key: C=Crannull, DD = DBWD 
 
Description Responsibility Timeframe 
Stage 1 - Refinements DD In Progress 
Stage 2 – Confirm Order C  
Stage 2 - Development DD 3 Weeks 
Stage 2 - Testing C & DD 1 Week 
Stage 2 - Launch C & DD  
Future Stages - Discussion C & DD  
 
What Next? 
 
If you have any questions or would like to order, please contact DBWD on 
questions@dannybarker.co.uk, 01462 819909. Or to order please complete and 
return the sales order on the following page. 8 
 
  SALES ORDER  
 DATE: OCTOBER 10, 2025 
DBWD 
Unit 8 
Clifton Bury Farm 
Bedfordshire 
SG17 5EX 
Phone: 01462 819909  
quote@dannybarker.co.uk 
 
 
TO Crannull ,  
Unit 4, Telmere Industrial Estate, Albert Rd, Luton LU1 3QF   
 
 
 
Signed:  ________________________________ 
 
Name:  ________________________________ 
 
Date:   ________________________________ 
Electronic Payments to: Sort code: 08-92-50 Account number: 68240174 
Cheques payable to Danny Barker Web Design Limited 
THANK YOU FOR YOUR BUSINESS. 
QTY ITEM DESCRIPTION UNIT COST LINE TOTAL 
1 Stage 1 Stage 1 (one off) £1600  
1 Stage 2 Stage 2 (one off) £2000  
1 Future Stages Future Stages £TBC  
   TOTAL TBC 
   INC VAT TBC

messages.content:
Here’s a quick overview of the files provided:

- **Crannull - AI Portals 2025 October.pdf [1]:** This document outlines a phased AI implementation plan for Crannull, prepared by DBWD. It details completed and upcoming stages of development, focusing on automation, data integration, and workflow optimization. Confirmed one‑off costs include £1,600 for Stage 1 and £2,000 for Stage 2, while future stage costs are still to be confirmed (total confirmed = £3,600) [1].  

- **iPhone15_vs_S24_comparison (1).xlsx [2]** and **iPhone15_vs_S24_comparison.xlsx [3]:** These spreadsheet files appear to be duplicates or variants of a device feature comparison between the iPhone 15 and Samsung Galaxy S24, though their content isn’t readable in the provided context.

- **iPhone15_vs_SamsungS24_comparison.csv [4]:** This file contains a detailed comparison of technical specifications between the iPhone 15 and the Samsung Galaxy S24, covering aspects such as processor, RAM, display, camera, battery, and charging capabilities [4].

Feature: Front Camera
iPhone 15: 12 MP TrueDepth
Samsung Galaxy S24: 12 MP Feature: Operating System
iPhone 15: iOS 17, upgradable to iOS 26.1
Samsung Galaxy S24: Android 14 with One UI 8.0 upgrade Feature: Dimensions
iPhone 15: 147.6 x 71.6 x 7.8 mm
Samsung Galaxy S24: 147 x 70.6 x 7.6 mm Feature: Processor
iPhone 15: Apple A16 Bionic 6-core CPU, 5-core GPU
Samsung Galaxy S24: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 (varies by region) Feature: Storage
iPhone 15: 128/256/512 GB NVMe
Samsung Galaxy S24: 128/256/512 GB UFS 3.1/4.0 Feature: RAM
iPhone 15: 6 GB
Samsung Galaxy S24: 8/12 GB Feature: Display
iPhone 15: 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR OLED, 2556 x 1179 px, 460 ppi, HDR, Dynamic Island
Samsung Galaxy S24: 6.2-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 1080 x 2340 px, 416 ppi, 120 Hz, HDR10+ Feature: Charging
iPhone 15: 20 W wired, 15 W MagSafe wireless
Samsung Galaxy S24: 25W wired, 15W wireless Feature: Rear Camera
iPhone 15: 48 MP Main, 12 MP Ultra Wide, 12 MP 2x Telephoto
Samsung Galaxy S24: 50 MP wide, 10 MP telephoto 3x optical zoom, 12 MP ultrawide Feature: Weight
iPhone 15: 171 g
Samsung Galaxy S24: 167-168 g Feature: Battery
iPhone 15: 3349 mAh, Up to 20 hours video playback
Samsung Galaxy S24: 4000 mAh, 25W wired, 15W wireless

last_assistant_message:
Here’s a quick overview of the files provided:

- **Crannull - AI Portals 2025 October.pdf [1]:** This document outlines a phased AI implementation plan for Crannull, prepared by DBWD. It details completed and upcoming stages of development, focusing on automation, data integration, and workflow optimization. Confirmed one‑off costs include £1,600 for Stage 1 and £2,000 for Stage 2, while future stage costs are still to be confirmed (total confirmed = £3,600) [1].  

- **iPhone15_vs_S24_comparison (1).xlsx [2]** and **iPhone15_vs_S24_comparison.xlsx [3]:** These spreadsheet files appear to be duplicates or variants of a device feature comparison between the iPhone 15 and Samsung Galaxy S24, though their content isn’t readable in the provided context.

- **iPhone15_vs_SamsungS24_comparison.csv [4]:** This file contains a detailed comparison of technical specifications between the iPhone 15 and the Samsung Galaxy S24, covering aspects such as processor, RAM, display, camera, battery, and charging capabilities [4].
